output.summary: Applied 3 fixes from Functional QA report: Removed manual attribute extraction in PHP (now uses auto-extracted variables from render_callback), added @var docblock annotations for all auto-extracted camelCase attribute variables, and removed unused postPermalink variable in TSX editor component.

changes: Lines 1-8: Updated file header docblock to include @var annotations for all auto-extracted attribute variables (selectedPost, isReversed, reverseRows, darkMode, buttonLabel, headingSize, eyebrow)
Removed lines 10, 22-27: Deleted manual attribute extraction ($selectedPost = $attributes['selectedPost'] ?? null; and all other $var = $attributes['key'] ?? default lines)
Line 20: Now directly uses $selectedPost from auto-extraction instead of manual extraction
filesModified.reasoning: The theme's render_callback (inc/helpers/blocks.php line 20) already calls extract($attributes, EXTR_SKIP), making all attributes available as local variables. Manual extraction was redundant and violated theme conventions. @var annotations document the auto-extracted variables and prevent PHP_CodeSniffer warnings for camelCase variable names.

changes: Line 70: Removed unused `const postPermalink = post?.link || '#';` variable
filesModified.reasoning: The editor preview doesn't render functional links (uses span.btn-primary instead of anchor tag), so the permalink is not used. Removing it eliminates the lint error.

buildStatus.details: Build completed successfully with no TypeScript errors

warningsDetails: Lines 12-13: Experimental WordPress component warnings for __experimentalToggleGroupControl and __experimentalToggleGroupControlOption (acceptable, standard pattern)
lintStatus.notes: No new errors introduced. The unused postPermalink lint error has been resolved. Remaining warnings in FeaturedPost are acceptable (experimental components).

syncStatus.details: TSX and PHP structure remain synchronized. PHP now uses auto-extracted variables following theme conventions. All conditional rendering, class names, and element order still match between TSX and PHP.

fixesApplied.description: Removed manual attribute extraction in PHP
fixesApplied.verification: PHP now uses auto-extracted variables from render_callback. Verified all attributes (selectedPost, isReversed, reverseRows, darkMode, buttonLabel, headingSize, eyebrow) are used directly without manual extraction.

fixesApplied.description: Added @var docblock for auto-extracted camelCase attribute variables
fixesApplied.verification: Docblock now documents all 7 auto-extracted attribute variables with proper types: selectedPost (int|null), isReversed (bool), reverseRows (bool), darkMode (bool), buttonLabel (string), headingSize (string), eyebrow (string). Also documents block and content variables.

fixesApplied.description: Removed unused postPermalink variable in TSX
fixesApplied.verification: Variable removed. Grep confirms no references to postPermalink in FeaturedPost.tsx. Lint error resolved.

notes: All 3 Functional QA issues resolved (2 major, 1 minor)
PHP now follows theme convention: uses auto-extracted attributes from render_callback
Build passes with no TypeScript errors
Lint errors reduced: unused postPermalink error eliminated
TSX/PHP sync maintained: all structure, classes, and conditional rendering patterns still match
Test page (ID 135) unaffected: changes are internal code quality improvements with no functional changes
FQA-004 (experimental component warnings) is acceptable per QA report - no action needed

verifications.details: PHP file no longer contains $var = $attributes['key'] ?? default patterns. All attributes used directly from auto-extraction.
File header includes @var annotations for all 7 attribute variables plus block and content variables
postPermalink variable no longer exists in TSX file, confirmed by grep
bun run build completed successfully with only expected asset size warnings
FeaturedPost lint errors reduced from 1 to 0 (unused variable error eliminated)
HTML structure, conditional rendering, and classes remain synchronized between editor and frontend
